releases.title: v0.3.1 — kana rendering fix + Safari hardening
releases.body:
## What's fixed in v0.3.1

A patch release that gets the v0.3.0 launch surfaces correct: kana actually look like kana now, Safari users on iOS / private mode no longer crash on page load, and the new mobile pages drop you straight into the practice instead of a hero section.

### ✍️ Kana strokes finally render correctly

The v0.3.0 kana writing practice and writing quiz pulled stroke data from KanjiVG, which ships *centerline* SVGs (`fill:none;stroke-width:3`) — single-line paths through the middle of each stroke. hanzi-writer expects *closed filled stroke outlines* + median waypoints, so feeding it centerlines made the renderer try to clip a thick polyline against open thin curves. Result: kana looked recognizable but mangled.

This release switches to **animCJK**, which already publishes data in hanzi-writer's exact CharacterData shape. The conversion script collapses to "fetch JSONL, filter to our 154 glyphs, transcribe verbatim" — when the source format already matches the target, you don't need a converter. Every hiragana, katakana, dakuten, and extended-katakana glyph now renders as a clean handwritten brushstroke matching its system-font reference.

License audit: animCJK kana data ships under LGPL-3.0-or-later — cleared via `manualOverride` in `data/data-licenses.json` (LGPL on static data only requires shipping the license text + attribution + allowing modification, all of which we satisfy via the SPDX-style header in the JSON, the data-licenses manifest, and the `/credits` page).

### 🍎 Safari Web Speech API no longer crashes the page

iOS Safari (and Safari with content blockers / private browsing) was crashing on every page load because `lib/tts.ts` touched `speechSynthesis.getVoices()` and added a `voiceschanged` listener at module-evaluation time. Both calls can throw or hang on those Safari surfaces.

All speech-API access is now fully lazy: a `getSynth()` wrapper with try/catch returns null on failure, voice-list refresh and the `voiceschanged` listener wire on first call inside a user-gesture handler, and `speakJapanese()` truncates utterances to 200 chars (Safari hard-crashes on very long inputs) and only calls `cancel()` when something is actually `speaking || pending` (some Safari versions crash on a no-op cancel).

### 📱 Mobile preview lands above the fold on /draw and /kana-write

On 390×844 viewports (iPhone-class), the v0.3.0 chrome — h1, intro paragraph, level/script/section selector card — pushed the actual stroke-order preview below the fold. Tighter mobile chrome: smaller heading on small viewports, intro paragraph hidden below `sm` (it's filler on mobile), shorter card padding, smaller margins between sections. Desktop layout unchanged.

### ♿ Accessibility — pre-existing /quiz violation closed

The 漢字 brand mark in the navbar used `text-vermilion` (`#c8553d`), which fails WCAG 2.1 AA at 4.03:1 against the cream background. Switched to `text-vermilion-deep` (`#a3402b`) — passes at 4.5:1.

axe-core was the source of truth: it flags color-contrast even on `aria-hidden` elements because sighted low-vision users still see them.

---

### Verification

- `bun run build` — strict TypeScript + Vite, gating deploys
- `bun run a11y` — Playwright + axe-core across **15 routes** including 0 violations on `/draw`, `/kana-write`, `/kana-write-quiz`, and `/credits`
- `bun run check:licenses` — npm production tree allowlist + bundled-data manifest, both clean
- `bun run check:unused` — knip; only the 3 intentional shadcn library-surface exports remain
- i18n parity — 624 EN keys + 624 ID keys, full key-set match
- Visual verification of kana rendering across all 154 glyphs (hiragana basic + dakuten, katakana basic + dakuten + extended), each compared to its system-font reference

---

### Commits

- `2544b73` — fix(kanjiverse): close pre-existing /quiz a11y violation — 漢字 brand mark vermilion → vermilion-deep
- `0660908` — fix(kanjiverse): kana stroke medians sample on-curve points instead of Bezier control points (intermediate fix superseded by source switch below)
- `2b251ce` — fix(kanjiverse): harden Web Speech API for Safari — defer all access until first call
- `a8abbfc` — fix(kanjiverse): kanji preview lands above the fold on mobile for /draw and /kana-write
- `304d300` — fix(kanjiverse): switch kana stroke data from KanjiVG to animCJK for proper hanzi-writer rendering
- `20bc9da` — chore(kanjiverse): rebrand kana stroke source from KanjiVG to animCJK in user-facing copy

---

### Credits

Stroke data for hiragana and katakana now derives from [animCJK](https://github.com/parsimonhi/animCJK) (Copyright © 2016–2026 FM-SH and contributors), licensed LGPL-3.0-or-later for the kana subset. Attribution lives on the `/credits` page.

releases.title: v0.3.0 — flashcards, kana writing, EN/ID
releases.body:
## What's new in v0.3.0

A release that turns Kanjiverse from a recognition drill into a complete daily-study companion. Spaced repetition, kana writing practice, full bilingual support, and a navbar that scales to it all.

### ✦ Spaced-repetition flashcard review

A new **/review** route ships proper SM-2 spaced repetition for kanji, hiragana, and katakana. Three-button self-grading (Again / Good / Easy → SM-2 quality 1 / 4 / 5), keyboard shortcuts (Space reveals; 1 / 2 / 3 grade), per-type filters (kanji + JLPT level, or specific kana script). When the queue empties, the page actually says "All caught up" — no fake busywork — with an opt-in "Pull 10 ahead-of-schedule" for users who want more.

The same SM-2 schedule powers the existing kanji / vocab / kana quizzes — answering anywhere feeds one shared review queue. Existing users get sensibly-scheduled cards on first open: a v1 → v2 IDB migration replays each user's answer history through the algorithm so already-mastered items don't dump back into the "new" bucket.

### ✍️ Kana writing practice + recall quiz

Two new routes for the writing side of kana fluency:

- **/kana-write** — animated stroke order + practice canvas for hiragana and katakana (both scripts, all sections). Bundles ~85 KB of KanjiVG-derived stroke data (CC BY-SA 3.0; attribution on /credits) in its own code-split chunk so the landing page stays light.
- **/kana-write-quiz** — romaji prompt, blank canvas, draw the kana from memory. Uses hanzi-writer's quiz mode in a new "recall" configuration (no outline guide, auto-start). Stroke order is graded against KanjiVG. Performance feeds the same SM-2 schedule as recognition — a user strong at recognizing but weak at writing still gets the right cards surfaced.

### 🌏 Bilingual UI — English + Bahasa Indonesia

Every user-facing string now flows through `t(key, vars?)`. Locale switcher lives in **/profile** (Language card). Kanji and vocabulary meanings switch with the locale; `<html lang>` updates per locale; missing translations fall back to English at runtime (soft failure rather than crash). Search filters match against both languages so users in ID locale can still type "water" and find 水 — and vice versa.

### 🌐 Cloudflare geo-detect for first-paint locale

Nginx writes a `cf_country` cookie from the Cloudflare `CF-IPCountry` header on `/index.html` (not on cached `/assets/*`, so the cookie can't get pinned to the wrong country). On first paint, `lib/geo-detect.ts` reads the cookie synchronously — a connection from Indonesia boots straight into the ID locale instead of flashing English. Once a user picks a locale (or the first choice persists), the geo signal is never re-applied, so travel doesn't reset their preference.

### 🧭 Navbar IA redesign — five top-level slots → three

The previous navbar had **Kanji · Quiz▾ · Review · Draw▾ · Reference▾** plus right-side meta. After this release's additions it would have grown crowded; instead it collapsed cleanly:

```
[漢字 Kanji]   ✦ Review · Learn ▾ · Browse ▾                │   🔥 ◯ ⌥
```

- **Review** — promoted to top-level (it's the daily-driver flow)
- **Learn ▾** — recognition (Kanji & Vocab Quiz, Kana Quiz, Kana Writing Quiz) and writing (Draw Kanji, Draw Kana), each in its own labelled sub-section
- **Browse ▾** — Kanji, Vocabulary, Kana, plus Find by Drawing (under "Tools")

The asymmetric standalone "Kanji" link is gone — every list page is now reached the same way. The "by Insomnius" tagline drops from the wordmark; it still appears on the landing page, footer, and `<meta>` where it does work.

### 📋 Virtualized list pages with IME-safe search

`/kanji-list` and `/vocab-list` ship `@tanstack/react-virtual` — even N1's full 2,000-row vocabulary scrolls smoothly on low-end Android. Search input is IME-aware (Japanese composition events don't trigger filtering mid-syllable) and keyboard-friendly: press `/` or `⌘K` to focus, `Escape` to clear.

### 🔧 Performance tooling

Three new visualizations make perf regressions easy to spot:

- `bun run check:bundle` — interactive treemap (`rollup-plugin-visualizer`) of every chunk
- `bun run check:lighthouse` — `@lhci/cli` against `/`, `/quiz`, `/kanji-list` with perf ≥ 90, a11y ≥ 95, LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, TBT < 300ms assertions
- **why-did-you-render** — opt-in render-cause inspector behind `VITE_WDYR=1 bun run dev`, tree-shaken from prod

### 🗺️ Activity tracking surfaced on /review

The review route now renders `QuizMeter` above the flashcard — same engagement strip that runs on the quizzes. Session correct/total + accuracy, daily-goal progress bar, streak chip with at-risk warning. The data was already being recorded under the hood (every grade calls `recordAnswer`); this just makes it visible while you're reviewing.

### 🔒 No brand references

Route copy, component naming, and code comments use "flashcard" / "spaced-repetition" rather than the Anki trademark. The `open-anki-jlpt-decks` attribution stays — that MIT-licensed data source's license requires it.

### ♿ Accessibility, performance, and tooling

- **15 routes** pass WCAG 2.1 AA (axe-core) — `/kana-write`, `/kana-write-quiz`, `/review` added to the harness; 630 passes, 1 pre-existing violation unchanged.
- **CLAUDE.md** gained explicit i18n rules and an unbreakable "always update credits" rule. The license-audit script runs as a deploy gate; the data-license manifest covers KanjiVG (CC BY-SA 3.0), with attribution surfaced on `/credits`.
- **Schema v2 migration policy** documented in `docs/BACKUP_SCHEMA.md` — every backup ever exported must keep importing into the current app, including the SM-2 history-replay derivation.
- The `Recognition`, `Writing`, and `Tools` sub-section labels in nav dropdowns use Newsreader italic small-caps with `bg-sumi/10` separators — staying inside the warm cream palette instead of shadcn's default `slate-200`.

---

15 routes pass WCAG 2.1 AA (axe-core), 630 checks, 1 pre-existing violation (the QuizTour modal's font-jp 漢字 stamp on `/quiz`, unrelated to this release). Nothing leaves your browser — no analytics, no tracking, no accounts, no telemetry.

releases.title: v0.2.0 — radical breakdown, similar kanji, native TTS
releases.body:
## What's new in v0.2.0

A release focused on closing dictionary-experience gaps and quietly raising the polish floor. Ten themes:

### 🎨 Find a kanji by drawing it

A new **Find by drawing** route (`/draw-search`) lets you sketch a character on a canvas and surfaces the closest matches from the JLPT N1–N5 set. Order-invariant stroke matching covers 1,978 of 2,034 kanji (97% — the 56 unsearchable ones are Japanese-only variants without upstream stroke data).

### 🔍 Visually-similar kanji on every detail panel

Every kanji detail page now has a **Similar** tab listing the 6 closest visually-related kanji, ranked by stroke shape with the same matcher `/draw-search` uses. Helps disambiguate easily-confused pairs (末 vs 未, 土 vs 士, 大 vs 太, …) right inside the detail you're already reading.

### 🧩 Radical / component breakdown

Every kanji detail now shows a **Parts** section listing the radical components it's built from (e.g. 語 = 言 + 五 + 口 + …). Each component links to its own kanji detail page when we have one, so you can dive into the building blocks. Data sourced from KRADFILE (CC BY-SA 4.0, EDRDG) — 100% of our 2,034 kanji are covered: 1,966 are decomposed into smaller pieces, and the remaining 68 (atomic radicals like 水, 火, 木, 心) get a meaningful "this kanji is itself a radical — a building block other kanji are made from" note instead of a false-negative empty state.

### 🔎 Search across every JLPT level at once

The `/kanji-list` search box used to filter only within the active level tab — searching "ocean" on N5 silently missed N3's 海. Now search results unify across all levels, sorted N5 → N1, with a JLPT-level badge on each result. Search also matches against on/kun romaji ("umi", "kai") in addition to kana forms.

### 🪶 Editorial segmented control on every list page

The N5–N1 level switcher on `/kanji-list` and `/vocab-list` and the Hiragana / Katakana switcher on `/kana-reference` are now the same Newsreader-letterform-with-vermilion-underline control the quiz home uses — consistent typography across the app, with descriptive labels under each level (Beginner / Elementary / Intermediate / Upper-int. / Advanced) instead of bare codes.

### 🗣️ Native Japanese pronunciation

Speaker buttons next to every kanji glyph, on/kun reading, vocab word, kana, and example sentence. Uses the user's OS Japanese voice via the Web Speech API — no audio files, no API keys, no server, works offline. Self-hides when no Japanese voice is installed (common on Linux). Mutable from the Profile page; respects `prefers-reduced-motion`.

### 🎯 Quiet gamification

- **Per-character mastery badges** on every kanji / vocab / kana detail page — neutral pill once you've seen a character, gold once you've mastered it (≥3 attempts, ≥80% accuracy).
- **Stealth SRS** — quiz pickers now lightly bias toward characters you got wrong recently and away from ones you just got right. No review queue, no due dates, no scheduling pressure.
- **Milestone celebrations** — a small gold toast appears when you cross a streak milestone (3 / 7 / 14 / 30 / 50 / 100 / 200 / 365) or a total-answered milestone (10 / 50 / 100 / 500 / 1000 / 2500 / 5000 / 10000). Fires only on a *correct* answer, exactly once per threshold, then never again.
- **Quiz feedback sounds** — a soft chime on correct, a low thud on incorrect, a fuller tone for milestones. Procedural via Web Audio API, zero asset weight, mutable from the Profile page, and silenced automatically under `prefers-reduced-motion`.

### ⌨️ Quiz Enter key now advances reliably

Pressing Enter after submitting an answer used to occasionally get "stuck" because focus dropped onto a disabled button. Focus now moves deliberately to **Next Question** when feedback appears, so a follow-up Enter advances cleanly — and screen readers announce "Next Question, button" so non-sighted users get the same cue.

### 📚 N1 vocabulary expanded from 10 entries to 2,685

Imported the full open-source N1 list from [open-anki-jlpt-decks](https://github.com/jamsinclair/open-anki-jlpt-decks) (MIT). The N1 quiz now feels like an actual N1 drill. Macron-style romaji (gakkō, kuruma) is generated at build time via wanakana.

### 🪪 Credits & data-source attribution

A new `/credits` page (linked from the footer) lists every third-party data source and typeface with license badge, attribution, upstream URL, and any audit note — generated directly from `data/data-licenses.json` so it stays in sync.

### ♿ Accessibility, performance, and tooling

- Contribution calendar cells are now real `<button>` elements with per-day aria-labels — keyboard, touch, and screen-reader users get the same per-day count that mouse hover gave.
- "Practice writing this kanji" links from the kanji detail to `/draw?char=X`. Each list page (`/kanji-list`, `/vocab-list`, `/kana-reference`) gained a "Related actions" nav row.
- Maskable PWA icons (192/512), well inside the 80% safe-area spec.
- Site-wide footer with Insomnius attribution and the new credits link.
- `bun run check:licenses` audits every npm package in the production-dependency tree against an SPDX allowlist, and validates every bundled / runtime-fetched data source against the curated manifest.
- **Bundle perf:** splitting `kanji-stroke-features.json` for the Similar-kanji tab also dropped the `/draw-search` chunk from **472 KB → 9 KB** and the PWA precache from **2,748 KB → 2,276 KB** — first-visit installs are ~470 KB lighter.

---

12 routes pass WCAG 2.1 AA (axe-core), 500 checks across the suite, 0 violations. Nothing leaves your browser — no analytics, no tracking, no accounts.
